title: The Good-natured Grey Gull
content:
The Good-natured Grey Gull,
    who carried the Old Owl, and his Crimson Carpet-bag,
    across the river, because he could not swim.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Edward Lear, titled "The Good-natured Grey Gull", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
